send_invoice: Send an invoice to the customer.
download_invoice: Download an invoice as a PDF.
get_order: Returns order summary fields including status, total, item count, and timestamps. Excludes line items, addresses, and tax/discount details.
fetch_order: Returns the full order record including line items, addresses, and payment method, providing more detailed data than get_order.
load_order: Returns a cached snapshot of the order with status, total, and item count, valid up to 5 minutes, optimized for high-read-volume scenarios.
retrieve_order: Returns the order with discount and tax calculations applied, providing subtotal, discount_amount, tax_amount, final_total, and discounts_applied. Computes final price rather than returning raw field values.
update_invoice: Applies a partial update to specified fields. Does not create a new invoice record, only modifying existing fields.
upsert_invoice: Creates or updates an invoice; creates the record if not found.
patch_invoice: Applies JSON Patch (RFC 6902) operations to an invoice and returns the count of applied operations.
replace_invoice: Replaces an invoice by overwriting all provided fields with the given values, setting unspecified fields to null; returns a minimal confirmation dict with the invoice ID and a replaced flag. Fully replaces the invoice without preserving the original, unlike tools that create amendments or drafts.
amend_invoice: Amends an invoice by creating a new amended invoice with a unique ID, marks the original as SUPERSEDED, and preserves it in the audit trail without modifying or deleting the original.
delete_ticket: Soft-deletes a ticket by setting a deleted_at timestamp, leaving the record recoverable.
archive_ticket: Moves the ticket to the archive store, marking it as archived and recoverable via restore_ticket. Does not delete data, instead moving it to a separate partition.
purge_ticket: Permanently removes the ticket from all stores, making the record irrecoverable. Irreversible deletion with no recovery option.
expire_ticket: Sets the ticket status to EXPIRED while keeping it fully visible and queryable, with data not removed. Unlike deletion or purging tools, it does not erase or hide ticket records.
search_accounts: Performs full-text search across account name, notes, contact email, and address fields, returning ranked results ordered by relevance score.
filter_accounts: Filters accounts by exact status, type, and region values, returning paginated results. Structured field-value filtering without text search.
query_accounts: Constructs a parameterized SQL-like WHERE clause query for accounts, supporting complex conditions with AND, OR, NOT, and IS NULL checks.
find_account: Looks up an account by external reference and returns a dictionary with the external reference and account data if found, or 404 if not found. Returns a dictionary with the external reference and account data if found, or 404 if not found.
lookup_account: Returns a dictionary containing the account_id and an account field set to None.
notify_customer: Sends an email notification to the customer's registered email address and confirms delivery via the EMAIL channel.
push_update: Sends a push notification via APNs or FCM to the customer's device, including customer ID, title, message, and marks the notification as delivered. Uses the PUSH channel and includes delivery confirmation in the response.
dispatch_sms: Sends an SMS to the customer's registered phone number via the SMS channel and returns a delivery status dictionary with `delivered` set to True.
message_customer: Creates an in-app message in the customer's notification inbox (visible in web and mobile dashboards) and does not send external notifications (email, push, or SMS). Limited to in-app channel only.
contact_customer: Routes a message via the customer's preferred contact channel (email if no preference is set) and returns the customer ID and resolved channel.
confirm_order: Changes an order's status from PENDING to CONFIRMED, sends a confirmation email to the customer, and returns the updated order details with status and email confirmation flag. Sends a customer-facing confirmation email as part of the order status transition.
approve_order: Marks an order as approved by recording the approver's ID and setting the status to APPROVED without notifying the customer. Unlike other order actions, it does not trigger customer notifications or external workflows.
fulfill_order: Changes order status to FULFILLED and triggers a warehouse pickup request. Does NOT capture payment — payment must have been captured separately.
process_order: Captures payment, checks inventory availability, reserves stock, and updates order status to PROCESSING in an atomic operation. Returns detailed status indicators including payment and inventory reservation results.
schedule_invoice: Schedules an invoice for automatic generation at a specified future datetime without creating a record, returning a schedule ID and status. Returns a schedule ID and status instead of invoice details, with the generate_at timestamp included in the response.
queue_invoice: Queues an invoice for asynchronous processing and returns a job ID, status, and estimated wait time.
stage_invoice: Creates an invoice in STAGED state for reviewer approval, returning a fixed invoice ID and status with requires_approval set to True.
draft_invoice: Creates a draft invoice with editable status and a predefined invoice ID.
